column3: Python is a high-level, interpreted, object-oriented programming language known for its simplicity and readability.
Object-Oriented Programming is a programming paradigm based on objects, classes, inheritance, encapsulation, polymorphism, and abstraction.
A class is a blueprint for creating object.
A object is a instance of a class.
Encapsulation is a process of binding data and mehtods into a single unit while restricting direct access.
Inheritance allows one class to acquire properties and methods of another class.
Polymorphism allows the same interface to perform different behaviors depending on the object.
Abstraction hides implementation details and exposes only essential features.
Exception handling manages runtime errors using try except else and finally blocks.
A metaclass defines how classes are created and customized in Python.
oop is a programming technique used to reduce code by creating classes and objects.
